project.name: og
project.summary: URL-driven Open Graph image generator for producing GitHub-oriented social preview banners with documented themes and deployment metadata.
goals: Generate Open Graph images and social preview banners from documented URL parameters.
Keep route handling, theme definitions, package metadata, and deployment behavior coherent as one product surface.
Expose only documented image-generation, theme, package, and workflow surfaces to consumers.
nonGoals: Own enterprise doctrine, project manifest schema, org rulesets, or shared CI and release policy.
Own downstream product branding strategy or hidden repository-specific marketing copy for another project.
Own GitHub repository data semantics beyond inputs that are rendered into generated images.

owns.name: og-image-service
owns.description: HTTP route behavior, request parameters, and response rendering for generated Open Graph images.

owns.name: og-theme-catalog
owns.description: Repository-local theme definitions, visual presets, and image composition logic.

owns.name: og-delivery
owns.description: Package metadata and central release workflow wiring for publishing the generator.

doesNotOwn: Enterprise doctrine, project manifest schema, rollout issue reconciliation, org rulesets, or shared release workflow implementation.
Product-specific branding requirements for downstream repositories unless represented as documented generator inputs.
GitHub platform data semantics, repository analytics, or customer-specific social marketing strategy.

allowedDependencies.surface: project manifest schema, standards, and rollout audit

forbiddenCouplings: Do not embed another repository's private branding assumptions as hidden generator behavior.
Do not require consumers to depend on repository-internal theme implementation details instead of documented route parameters.
Do not treat a source revert as complete recovery after an image generator deployment has been published without runtime readback or smoke proof.
Do not publish enterprise process policy from this repository instead of SylphxAI/doctrine.

delivery.deployPath: Main-branch pushes call .github/workflows/release.yml, which delegates release handling to the central SylphxAI/.github reusable workflow.
delivery.productionProof: Production changes require deployment/readback evidence plus generated-image smoke evidence for affected route parameters or themes; this proof is not yet formalized.

gaps.description: Declare pull-request validation or intentionally document why route/theme changes rely on external validation before full adoption.
Document deployment readback and generated-image smoke proof required for production image route or theme changes.
Add runbooks for deployment verification, route smoke checks, and forward-fix recovery.
